actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (Straight Arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand on the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean slightly forward while gripping the handles.
2.Engage your core and back, pulling the handles down until your arms swing back past your thighs, keeping the arms straight.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale while pulling, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the grips to the high position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Position your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees, and grip the handles with your hands
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: The knees bend and extend in a smooth motion, while the hips push back and descend.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms straight, swing them up and down, and extend your arms alongside your hips.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down towards your hips, engage the muscles of your back and lower body.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Maintain an upright posture with your back straight.\n2.Keep a slight bend in the knees and hips.\n3.Slightly lean forward while gripping the handles.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673145702.jpg"},{"context":"1.Engage your core and back muscles, pulling the handles down until your arms swing past your thighs. Keep your arms straight throughout the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673149182.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down towards your hips, engage the muscles of your back and lower body.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms straight, swing them up and down, and extend your arms alongside your hips.

actionLibraryList.title: Bodyweight Alternating Forward Lunges
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start standing with your core engaged. Step one leg forward into a lunge, lowering your body until the front knee is aligned with your toes and the back knee is close to the ground without touching it. 
2.Exhale as you push back to standing through the front foot, then switch legs and repeat. Keep your torso upright and your hips facing forward. 
3.Avoid letting your front knee cave inward or extend beyond your toes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749786633555.jpg","context":"1.Align the front knee with the toes of the front foot.\n2.Lower the rear knee toward the ground without letting it touch the floor."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749786679773.jpg","context":"1.Push through the front heel to return to standing, then switch legs and repeat."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you stand up, inhale as you lower down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel strong activation in your legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees cave inward or extend past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Alternating Reverse Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.
2.Keep both knees pointing forward.
3.Step one leg back and lower until both knees are at 90°.
4.Return to standing, then alternate to the other leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Step one leg back into a single-leg squat.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep both knees bent at ninety degrees.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep both knees pointing straight forward.\n2.Step one leg back and lower until both knees are at 90°.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749800497307.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees go past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Dynamic Shoulder Circles
actionLibraryList.context: Lift your head, straighten your chest, and perform shoulder circles to the fullest range.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Touch your fingers to your shoulders and draw circles using your shoulders as the pivot point.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749524137343.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement can enhance the mobility of your shoulder joint.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your body still and avoid swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Rotator Cuff Stimulation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand and keep your upper arms parallel to the ground as you open up.                                                                                 2.The elbow joint is the fixed point for external rotation; keep your elbow at a 90-degree angle throughout the movement.                                    
3.Keep breathing and activate your rotator cuff.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Stand in a neutral position.\n2.Keep your upper arms parallel to the ground as you open up.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749717912465.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749717915966.jpg","context":"1.Using the elbow as the pivot point, perform an external rotation while maintaining a 90-degree angle at the elbow joint."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement can activate your shoulder rotator cuff muscles.

actionLibraryList.title: Dynamic Hamstring Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your upper body upright and bend forward as far as you can.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1753410285189.jpg","context":"1.Front leg straight, dorsiflex the foot.\n2.Back leg slightly bent.\n3.Shift your weight onto the back leg."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel the stretch in the back of your thighs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Kneeling Dual-Handle Chest Press
actionLibraryList.context: Kneel on both knees with handles in each hand, palms facing down, elbows slightly below shoulder level and slightly flared out. Keep your chest lifted, abs engaged, spine neutral, and core tight. Inhale to prepare. As you exhale, Push both hands forward until the arms are almost straight but without locking the elbows, focusing on squeezing the chest muscles. Inhale as you slowly return to the starting position with control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748920630537.jpg","context":"1.Hold one handle in each hand with palms facing downward.\n2.Keep your elbows slightly below shoulder level and gently flared outward."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748920633866.jpg","context":"1.Push both hands forward until the arms are almost straight but without locking the elbows."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during exertion, inhale during the return.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on contracting the middle portion of the pectoralis major.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your core engaged to prevent lower back sagging or leaning forward.

actionLibraryList.title: Incline Tripod Row
actionLibraryList.context: Drive the movement with your elbow joint.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749009855836.jpg","context":"1.Engage your core, keeping your back flat and neutral.\n2.Grip the handle with one hand, palm facing inward, while the other hand rests on the incline bench."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749009860150.jpg","context":"1.Drive the movement with your elbow, pulling the handle towards the side of your abdomen."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on engaging your back muscles during the movement.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your lower back straight and avoid collapsing it throughout the exercise.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grip to ground level.
2.Grab the handles with both hands and bring them to an upright position.
3.Lift your chest, keep your back straight, and engage your core for stability.
4.Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
5.Push your hips back to initiate the squat, keeping your back straight.
6.Maintain balance throughout the squat, returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley block to the footboard end with both handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't arch back when you return to the straight. Don't thrust your hips forward at the top of the motion.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Big breath at the start of the movement, engaging your glutes, slowly exhale during the ascent.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Initiate standing movement by squeezing your glutes and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your arms naturally extended.\n2.Hips higher than the knees.\n3.Align your knees with your toes.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750233975187.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep the knees straight, but do not lock them.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750234126578.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you exert force, inhale as you return.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your back as you return to the upright position. Do not over-extend your lower back at the peak contraction.

actionLibraryList.title: Single Handle Duck Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly turned out, holding the handle with both hands.
2.Squat down by bending at the hips and knees, keeping your knees aligned with your toes.
3.Shift your weight slightly toward your heels, keeping your chest lifted.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749090148374.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, toes slightly turned out, holding the handle with both hands."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1767772024889.jpg","context":"1.Bend your knees outward, tracking in line with your toes as you squat."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you drive upward, inhale as you lower down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel strong engagement in your legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees from caving inward or extending past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Bulgarian Split Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Grab a handle in each hand and set up in a lunge stance with one foot supported on the bench behind you.
3.Squat with your front leg and lean forward slightly until your thigh is parallel to the ground. Lower your rear knee towards the floor.
4.Keep your back straight, engage your hips and legs, and push through your heels to stand up.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Keep your back upright.
2.Set up in a lunge stance with one foot supported on the bench behind you.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751508616776.jpg"},{"context":"1.Grab a handle in each hand.
2.Squat with your front leg and lean forward slightly until your thigh is parallel to the ground.
3.Lower your rear knee towards the floor.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751508623015.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe naturally, exhaling as you rise and inhaling as you lower.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your quads and press through your leading leg to stand back up.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Dual-Handle Toes-In Calf Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed inward in a slight ‘V’ position.
2.Hold the handles naturally at your sides, engage your core, and keep your back straight.
3.Keep your knees slightly bent and lift your heels off the ground, rising onto the balls of your feet to reach the highest point.
4.Pause at the top for 1–2 seconds, feeling the contraction in your calves.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934310434.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned inward in a “duck foot” stance."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934314090.jpg","context":"1.Lift your heels off the ground and rise onto your toes as high as possible, focusing on contracting the outer calves."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid using momentum or swaying your body.

actionLibraryList.title: Bench Supine Cable Scissor Kick
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Lie supine on the flat bench, lift your head off the bench, slightly tuck your chin, and secure your legs with ankle straps.
2.Tighten your core and maintain abdominal tension.
3.You can hold onto the frame for stability during the exercise.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the cable pulley to the ground-level position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lie on your back on a flat bench and secure the ankle straps.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Perform leg abductions and adductions alternately.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Maintain full activation and tension in your abdominal muscles throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Secure the foot straps and execute alternating scissor kicks.\n2.Tighten your core and maintain abdominal tension.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749453125949.jpg"},{"context":"1.You can hold onto the frames for stability during the exercise.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749453130247.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Tighten your core and maintain abdominal tension.
